This video is a long, meandering conversation about the economics of
open-source maintainership. There is no overview heading, no index, and no
labeled fields — just prose, the way a human might take notes.

The guest argues three things:

1. Sustainable funding beats sporadic donations
2. *Corporate* sponsorship changes maintainer incentives
3. Burnout is a structural problem, not a personal one

> "Nobody pays for the plumbing until it leaks."

Later the discussion drifts into packaging ecosystems (`npm`, `cargo`, and
friends) and how supply-chain incidents shifted attitudes toward vendoring.
Some sentences run long; some are fragments. Markdown emphasis appears
**inconsistently** and _sparsely_.

Final thought — the host recommends an essay on maintainer economics and
wraps up without any summary section at all.
